title: NGSS Science Grade 7
description: A Grade 7 NGSS path for systems thinking, cells, photosynthesis, respiration, evidence-based explanations, and model-driven practice.

lessons.title: NGSS G7 Science 1: Systems, Scale, and Models
lessons.description: Use systems thinking, scale, and models to explain complex science phenomena.
questions: A science system is best described as...
a group of interacting parts that work together
a group of interacting parts that work together
A system has components and relationships, such as organs in a body system or parts of an ecosystem.
In a system model, matter, energy, or information entering the system is called an ____.
It goes into the system before the process happens.
Inputs enter a system; outputs leave it after processes happen.
A model can be useful even if it does not show every detail of the real system.
Good models are useful because they highlight important parts while leaving out some details.
Select the items that could be components of an ecosystem system model.
Choose parts that interact in the ecosystem.
Sunlight, plants, and animals can all be components in an ecosystem model.
Put these model-building steps in a strong order.
Use evidence to revise the model
Use evidence to revise the model
Start by deciding what is inside the system.
Scientists first define the boundary, then add parts, interactions, and revisions from evidence.
Why does scale matter when studying cells, organs, and ecosystems?
Scale never changes what we notice
Only the largest scale is scientific
A cell-scale model shows different evidence than an organism-scale or ecosystem-scale model.
The line that separates what is inside a system model from what is outside is the system ____.
It marks the edge of the model.
A system boundary helps scientists decide what to include and what to treat as surroundings.
Which statements are good model-evaluation questions?
What does the model explain well?
How can I make it prettier only?
What does the model explain well?
Evaluation is about explanation and evidence.
Scientists judge models by usefulness, limits, and evidence.
A food web is a model mainly because it...
is a perfect copy of nature
proves every ecosystem is the same
A food web focuses on relationships.
Food webs model feeding relationships and energy flow, but they do not show every detail.
Choose a familiar system, such as a school, a plant, or a phone. Name two components, one input, one process, and one output.
Use the words component, input, process, and output.
A strong response identifies parts and explains how something enters, changes, and leaves the system.

reviewAdvice.rewatchMessage: Rewatch the systems, scale, and models lesson and focus on the question types you missed.

questions.prompt: A science system is best described as...
choices: a group of interacting parts that work together
questions.answer: a group of interacting parts that work together
questions.hint: Look for parts plus interactions.
questions.explanation: A system has components and relationships, such as organs in a body system or parts of an ecosystem.

encouragement.incorrect: Good attempt. Use the hint and keep building the model.

questions.hint: It goes into the system before the process happens.
questions.explanation: Inputs enter a system; outputs leave it after processes happen.

questions.hint: All models simplify reality.
questions.explanation: Good models are useful because they highlight important parts while leaving out some details.

encouragement.incorrect: Good attempt. Use the hint and keep building the model.

questions.hint: Choose parts that interact in the ecosystem.
questions.explanation: Sunlight, plants, and animals can all be components in an ecosystem model.

encouragement.incorrect: Good attempt. Use the hint and keep building the model.

choices: Use evidence to revise the model
answer: Use evidence to revise the model

questions.explanation: Scientists first define the boundary, then add parts, interactions, and revisions from evidence.

choices: is a perfect copy of nature
proves every ecosystem is the same
questions.hint: A food web focuses on relationships.
questions.explanation: Food webs model feeding relationships and energy flow, but they do not show every detail.

lessons.title: NGSS G7 Science 2: Cells Are the Basic Unit of Life
lessons.description: Explain why cells are the basic unit of life and how cell structures support life functions.
lessons.focus: cells as the basic unit of life
questions: Why are cells called the basic unit of life?
All living things are made of cells that carry out life functions
Cells are always larger than organs
Cells do not interact with their environment
All living things are made of cells that carry out life functions
Cells are the smallest units that can perform life processes.
The cell structure that controls what enters and leaves the cell is the cell ____.
The cell membrane helps regulate materials moving into and out of the cell.
A single-celled organism can carry out all life functions in one cell.
One cell can still be alive.
Many organisms are unicellular and perform nutrition, response, and reproduction within one cell.
Which are common needs or functions of living cells?
Cells need materials and energy and must manage waste.
Cells exchange materials, use energy, and remove wastes to stay alive.
Order these levels of organization from smallest to largest.
Start with the basic unit of life.
Cells build tissues, tissues build organs, and organs work in organ systems.
A microscope helps scientists study cells because cells are usually...
too small to see clearly with only our eyes
too small to see clearly with only our eyes
Microscopes extend observation to the cellular scale.
A specialized cell has a structure that helps it do a specific ____.
Cell structures often support the function the cell performs.
Which observations would be evidence that a sample is living?
Cellular structure and life processes support the claim that a sample is living.
Which claim best connects structure and function?
A nerve cell has long branches that help it send signals
A cell has no parts and no jobs
A tissue is smaller than a cell
A microscope changes nonliving things into cells
A nerve cell has long branches that help it send signals
Look for a feature helping a job.
Structure-function reasoning explains how a shape or part supports what the cell does.
Pick one cell structure from the lesson and explain how its structure helps the cell survive or do its job.
Name the structure, then explain the job.
A strong response connects a cell part to a life function with evidence-based reasoning.

reviewAdvice.rewatchMessage: Rewatch the cells as the basic unit of life lesson and focus on the question types you missed.

questions.explanation: Cells exchange materials, use energy, and remove wastes to stay alive.

questions.hint: Start with the basic unit of life.
questions.explanation: Cells build tissues, tissues build organs, and organs work in organ systems.

choices: too small to see clearly with only our eyes
questions.answer: too small to see clearly with only our eyes
questions.hint: Think about scale.
questions.explanation: Microscopes extend observation to the cellular scale.

questions.hint: Structure and function go together.
questions.explanation: Cell structures often support the function the cell performs.

questions.hint: Living things share life processes.

A cell has no parts and no jobs
A tissue is smaller than a cell
A microscope changes nonliving things into cells
questions.answer: A nerve cell has long branches that help it send signals
questions.hint: Look for a feature helping a job.
questions.explanation: Structure-function reasoning explains how a shape or part supports what the cell does.

lessons.title: NGSS G7 Science 3: Plant Cells and Animal Cells
lessons.description: Compare plant and animal cells using organelles, structure-function evidence, and models.
questions: Which structure is found in plant cells but not animal cells?
Plant cells have cell walls outside the membrane; animal cells do not.
Plant cells use ____ to capture light energy for photosynthesis.
Chloroplasts contain chlorophyll and help plants make sugar from light energy.
Both plant and animal cells have mitochondria.
Both plant and animal cells use mitochondria for cellular respiration.
Select structures commonly found in both plant and animal cells.
Cell membrane, nucleus, and mitochondria are common in both plant and animal cells.
Order these from general comparison to evidence-based conclusion.
Write a claim about plant vs animal cells
Write a claim about plant vs animal cells
A strong comparison moves from observation to evidence and claim.
A large central vacuole in a plant cell mainly helps with...
Large vacuoles store water and help plant cells maintain pressure.
The organelle that often acts as the cell control center is the ____.
The nucleus contains DNA and helps control cell activities.
Which observations support the claim that a cell is probably a plant cell?
Plant cells often have rigid walls and chloroplasts.
Cell wall and chloroplast evidence strongly support a plant-cell claim.
Why is a Venn diagram useful for comparing plant and animal cells?
It separates shared and unique structures
It proves cells are not alive
It only shows the largest organelles
It separates shared and unique structures
Venn diagrams compare similarities and differences.
A Venn diagram is a model for organizing shared and unique features.
Explain one similarity and one difference between plant and animal cells, using the name of at least one organelle.
Use one shared structure and one plant-only or animal-cell feature.
A strong response compares cells using specific structures such as chloroplast, cell wall, nucleus, or mitochondria.

reviewAdvice.rewatchMessage: Rewatch the plant cells and animal cells lesson and focus on the question types you missed.

questions.hint: Plants need rigid support.

It proves cells are not alive

questions.answer: It separates shared and unique structures

lessons.title: NGSS G7 Science 4: Photosynthesis: Light to Sugar
lessons.description: Model photosynthesis as a matter-and-energy process that turns light, carbon dioxide, and water into sugar and oxygen.
questions: What is the main purpose of photosynthesis?
Photosynthesis uses light energy to build sugar molecules.
Photosynthesis happens mainly in organelles called ____.
Chloroplasts capture light energy for photosynthesis.
Carbon dioxide and water are inputs for photosynthesis.
Plants use carbon dioxide and water, plus light energy, to make sugar and oxygen.
Water and carbon dioxide enter the plant
Water and carbon dioxide enter the plant
Light capture drives the rearrangement of matter into sugar and oxygen.
In a leaf disk experiment, disks floating can be evidence that...
Oxygen from photosynthesis can collect and help leaf disks float.
The green pigment that absorbs light energy is ____.
It gives many leaves their green color.
Chlorophyll absorbs light used in photosynthesis.
Which variables could affect the rate of photosynthesis?
the color of the notebook nearby
Choose variables connected to the process.
Light, carbon dioxide, and temperature can influence photosynthesis rate.
carbon dioxide + water + light -> sugar + oxygen
sugar + oxygen -> carbon dioxide + water + energy
carbon dioxide + water + light -> sugar + oxygen
The equation model summarizes matter and energy changes in photosynthesis.
A plant is placed in bright light and then in dim light. Predict how photosynthesis might change and explain your reasoning using inputs, outputs, or evidence.
Use light as the changing variable.
A strong response links light availability to sugar/oxygen production and explains the evidence you would look for.

reviewAdvice.rewatchMessage: Rewatch the photosynthesis lesson and focus on the question types you missed.

questions.prompt: What is the main purpose of photosynthesis?
questions.hint: Plants store energy in sugar.
questions.explanation: Photosynthesis uses light energy to build sugar molecules.

encouragement.incorrect: Good attempt. Use the hint and keep building the model.

questions.prompt: Photosynthesis happens mainly in organelles called ____.
questions.hint: They contain chlorophyll.
questions.explanation: Chloroplasts capture light energy for photosynthesis.

encouragement.incorrect: Good attempt. Use the hint and keep building the model.

questions.prompt: Carbon dioxide and water are inputs for photosynthesis.
questions.hint: Think reactants.
questions.explanation: Plants use carbon dioxide and water, plus light energy, to make sugar and oxygen.

encouragement.incorrect: Good attempt. Use the hint and keep building the model.

questions.prompt: Select the outputs of photosynthesis.
questions.hint: Outputs leave the process.
questions.explanation: Photosynthesis produces sugar and oxygen.

encouragement.incorrect: Good attempt. Use the hint and keep building the model.

questions.prompt: Order this simplified photosynthesis process.
choices: Water and carbon dioxide enter the plant
answer: Water and carbon dioxide enter the plant
questions.hint: Start with light capture.
questions.explanation: Light capture drives the rearrangement of matter into sugar and oxygen.

encouragement.incorrect: Good attempt. Use the hint and keep building the model.

questions.prompt: In a leaf disk experiment, disks floating can be evidence that...
questions.hint: Gas can make disks float.
questions.explanation: Oxygen from photosynthesis can collect and help leaf disks float.

encouragement.incorrect: Good attempt. Use the hint and keep building the model.

questions.prompt: The green pigment that absorbs light energy is ____.
questions.hint: It gives many leaves their green color.
questions.explanation: Chlorophyll absorbs light used in photosynthesis.

encouragement.incorrect: Good attempt. Use the hint and keep building the model.

questions.prompt: Which variables could affect the rate of photosynthesis?
choices: the color of the notebook nearby
questions.hint: Choose variables connected to the process.
questions.explanation: Light, carbon dioxide, and temperature can influence photosynthesis rate.

encouragement.incorrect: Good attempt. Use the hint and keep building the model.

questions.prompt: Which model best represents photosynthesis?
choices: carbon dioxide + water + light -> sugar + oxygen
sugar + oxygen -> carbon dioxide + water + energy
questions.answer: carbon dioxide + water + light -> sugar + oxygen
questions.hint: Recall the inputs and outputs.
questions.explanation: The equation model summarizes matter and energy changes in photosynthesis.

encouragement.incorrect: Good attempt. Use the hint and keep building the model.

questions.hint: Use light as the changing variable.

lessons.title: NGSS G7 Science 5: Cellular Respiration: Energy from Food
lessons.description: Explain how cells release usable energy from food through cellular respiration in mitochondria.
questions: What is the main purpose of cellular respiration?
Respiration helps cells use food energy.
Cellular respiration breaks down sugar to release energy cells can use.
Cellular respiration happens mainly in organelles called ____.
They are often called cell powerhouses.
Mitochondria help convert food energy into usable cellular energy.
Cellular respiration uses oxygen and glucose as inputs.
Most cellular respiration uses glucose and oxygen.
Select the outputs of cellular respiration.
Outputs are produced by the process.
Respiration produces carbon dioxide, water, and usable energy.
Order this simplified cellular respiration process.
Energy is released and carbon dioxide/water are produced
Energy is released and carbon dioxide/water are produced
Start with food entering the cell.
Cells use oxygen in mitochondria to break down glucose and release energy.
How are photosynthesis and cellular respiration related?
Photosynthesis stores energy in sugar; respiration releases energy from sugar
They are exactly the same process
Photosynthesis stores energy in sugar; respiration releases energy from sugar
One builds sugar, one breaks it down.
The two processes connect matter and energy in living systems.
The sugar molecule used as a fuel in respiration is often called ____.
It is the sugar made by photosynthesis.
Glucose is broken down during cellular respiration.
Which organisms carry out cellular respiration?
Plant, animal, and many single-celled organisms use cellular respiration.
During exercise, muscle cells need more energy. What process must increase?
Muscles need usable energy from food.
Active cells increase respiration to release more usable energy.
Compare photosynthesis and cellular respiration in two sentences. Include one input or output from each process.
Mention what each process uses or produces.
A strong response shows that photosynthesis builds sugar while respiration releases energy from sugar.
